sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 36-year-old female, presents with complaints of ankle pain following a fall two days ago.
She reports that her left ankle twisted underneath her on the outside, causing pain and swelling.
She has been using ibuprofen, ice, and elevation for pain and swelling, with minimal relief.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
Review of systems: The patient reports that the pain is localized to the outside of her ankle, with no pain radiating to her calf, lower leg, or knee.
Current medications: The patient is taking metformin for diabetes, norvasc for high blood pressure, and ibuprofen as needed for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 120/70.
Physical exam: The patient has tenderness over the lateral malleolus and the fifth metatarsal on her left ankle.
There is swelling and redness, with pain with valgus stressing of the ankle.
The patient has normal sensation and pulses in her ankle.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has an ankle sprain, and I am concerned for a possible fracture due to tenderness over the fifth metatarsal.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; possible fracture; diabetes; high blood pressure.
Plan:
I ordered an X-ray of the patient's ankle to rule out a fracture.
I will have her wear an air splint and use crutches until the X-ray results are available.
I prescribed naprosyn 500mg twice a day for pain control.
I will check her hemoglobin A1c and order baseline labs to monitor her diabetes.
I will refill her norvasc prescription and schedule a follow-up appointment to review her X-ray results and adjust her treatment plan as needed.
Follow-up: I will have the patient return in one week for follow-up to review her X-ray results and adjust her treatment plan accordingly.
